title: BSL or communication failure
header: I am writing to complain about a communication failure on a journey with {{operator}} on {{date}}. As a Deaf passenger who uses British Sign Language as my first language, the lack of an accessible communication channel left me unable to receive important journey information.
legalParagraph: Operators are required, under the Equality Act 2010, to make reasonable adjustments to ensure disabled passengers can access services on equal terms. The British Sign Language Act 2022 recognises BSL as a language of England, Wales and Scotland and informs how public-facing services should accommodate BSL users. The Accessible Travel Policy framework (overseen by the ORR for rail) also commits operators to accessible communication.
ask: I would like a full investigation, a written response within 20 working days, and confirmation of the alternative communication channels (such as Relay UK, SignVideo, or written-message support) that you will make available to BSL users in future.
